question: What are the exact daily, weekly, and monthly Analysis Window semantics, timezone, week start, partial-period behavior, and relationship between calendar-aligned and rolling comparisons?
context: PRD must choose one explicit v1 rule and validate assumption A6. Discovery recommends fixed Africa/Johannesburg local-date semantics, Monday-start calendar weeks clipped to the Budget Period, calendar days, and month-to-date through the As-of local date; rolling comparisons remain out unless representative evidence proves necessary. DST, leap day, future As-of, and partial-day behavior require acceptance examples.
decision: V1 freezes the As-of Instant from the trusted TimeProvider at operation start; it is returned in provenance and is not caller-controlled. Analysis uses fixed Africa/Johannesburg local dates. Day means the one calendar date containing the As-of Instant clipped to the Budget Period; week means Monday through Sunday clipped to the Budget Period; month means Budget Period start through the earlier of the As-of local date or period end, inclusive. A completed historical Budget Period uses the full month; a future period returns Not Started for elapsed-time metrics. Rolling windows are unsupported in v1. Effective Date controls membership, and As-of does not claim to reconstruct source state before later corrections.
